skill.name: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Distro for Python
skill.description: One-line setup for Application Insights with OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation. Use this skill to quickly configure Azure monitoring for Python applications with automatic telemetry collection for Flask, Django, FastAPI, and other popular frameworks.
skill.summary: Quickly configure Azure Application Insights monitoring with OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation for Python applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. The static analyzer detected no suspicious patterns. The skill provides reference documentation for the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Python package - a legitimate Microsoft monitoring library. No security risks identified.

content.user_title: Configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for Python
content.value_statement: Setting up application monitoring in Azure requires understanding connection strings, auto-instrumentation, and telemetry configuration. This skill provides ready-to-use code snippets and best practices for adding Application Insights monitoring to Python applications with minimal code changes.
actual_capabilities: Generate one-line Azure Monitor setup code for Python applications
Provide configuration examples for Flask, Django, FastAPI, and other frameworks
Show how to add custom traces, metrics, and logs to Application Insights
Explain sampling configuration and cloud role name setup
Demonstrate Azure AD authentication for production workloads
List supported auto-instrumentation libraries and their telemetry types
limitations: This skill provides reference documentation only - it does not execute code or create resources in Azure
Requires a valid Azure Application Insights connection string to function
Does not cover on-premise or non-Azure monitoring solutions
Auto-instrumentation coverage depends on the libraries used in the target application

use_cases.title: Add monitoring to a Flask web application
use_cases.description: Quickly add Application Insights telemetry to an existing Flask API with auto-instrumentation for HTTP requests and exceptions

use_cases.title: Set up production monitoring with AAD auth
use_cases.description: Configure Azure Monitor with Azure Active Directory authentication instead of instrumentation keys for secure production deployments

use_cases.title: Add custom business metrics to Application Insights
use_cases.description: Create custom counters, metrics, and trace attributes to monitor business-specific events alongside auto-collected telemetry

prompt_templates.title: Basic Azure Monitor setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to my Python application with just a few lines of code.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants minimal setup for basic Application Insights monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Configure monitoring for Flask app
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for a Flask application? Show me the complete setup including imports and initialization.
prompt_templates.scenario: Flask developer needs specific framework integration

prompt_templates.title: Add custom traces and metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add custom traces with custom attributes and custom metrics to Application Insights using OpenTelemetry.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs to track business-specific events

prompt_templates.title: Production configuration with AAD auth
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the best practice for configuring Azure Monitor in production using Azure AD authentication instead of connection strings?
prompt_templates.scenario: Security-conscious developer setting up production monitoring

output_examples.input: How do I add Azure Monitor to my Python FastAPI app?
output_examples.output:
Add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to your FastAPI app in just two steps:

1. Install the package:
pip install azure-monitor-opentelemetry

2. Add this at the top of your main.py:
from azure.monitor.opentelemetry import configure_azure_monitor
configure_azure_monitor()

This automatically instruments FastAPI, HTTP requests, and common libraries. Your application will now send traces, metrics, and logs to Application Insights.

output_examples.input: Show me how to track a custom business event
output_examples.output:
You can add custom traces with attributes to track business events:

from opentelemetry import trace
tracer = trace.get_tracer(__name__)

with tracer.start_as_current_span("checkout_processed") as span:
    span.set_attribute("order.total", 99.99)
    span.set_attribute("customer.tier", "premium")
    span.set_attribute("items.count", 3)

This creates a span in Application Insights with your custom business data, enabling you to filter and analyze by these attributes in your queries.

best_practices: Call configure_azure_monitor() before importing any instrumented libraries to ensure auto-instrumentation loads correctly
Use environment variables for connection strings in production instead of hardcoding them in source files
Set the cloud role name via the resource parameter to identify your service correctly in the Application Map
anti_patterns: Calling configure_azure_monitor() after importing instrumented libraries - this breaks auto-instrumentation
Hardcoding connection strings with instrumentation keys in source code - use environment variables instead
Enabling all instrumentations in low-memory environments - selectively enable only the frameworks you use

faq.question: What is Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry?
faq.answer: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry is a Microsoft distribution of OpenTelemetry that sends telemetry data to Azure Application Insights. It provides auto-instrumentation for popular Python frameworks with minimal code changes.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an Azure subscription with an Application Insights resource. The skill helps you write the code, but you must create the Azure resource and obtain a connection string.

faq.question: What frameworks are automatically instrumented?
faq.answer: The distro automatically instruments Flask, Django, FastAPI, Starlette, Requests, urllib3, httpx, aiohttp, psycopg2, pymysql, pymongo, and redis. Full list is in the documentation.

faq.question: Can I use this without instrumentation keys?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential instead of instrumentation keys. This is recommended for production environments.

faq.question: How do I filter or sample telemetry?
faq.answer: Use the sampling_ratio parameter in configure_azure_monitor() to set a value between 0.0 and 1.0. For example, sampling_ratio=0.1 sends 10% of telemetry.

faq.question: Does this work with serverless functions?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry works with Azure Functions. Add the configuration in your function app initialization code following the same patterns.
